59	
60	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
61	
62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
101	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
102	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
105	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
106	
107	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
108	
109	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
110	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
111	
112	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
113	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
114	
115	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
116	
117	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
118	
119	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
120	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
121	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
122	
123	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
124	
125	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
126	
127	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
128	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
129	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
130	discrete valuation on `f`.
131	
132	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
133	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
136	
137	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
138	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
139	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
140	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
141	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
142	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
143	
144	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
145	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
146	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
147	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
149	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
150	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
151	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
152	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
153	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
154	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
155	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
156	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
157	
158	end Scheme.RationalMap
159	
160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk
173	`O_{X,Y.point}`. The `[IsIntegral X]` precondition makes
174	`IsDomain (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` available so that the
175	`IsDiscreteValuationRing` predicate is well-formed
176	(`AlgebraicGeometry.instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`). -/
177	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) [IsIntegral X] : Prop where
178	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk is a discrete valuation
179	  ring. (This is the precise content of Hartshorne's `(*)`; the weaker
180	  `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` derives via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹
181	  `IsPrincipalIdealRing` ⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain via the bridge
182	  instance below.) -/
183	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
184	
185	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
186	synthesis can derive `IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for
187	every prime divisor `Y`. -/
188	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instIsDiscreteValuationRingStalk
189	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
190	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
191	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
192	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
193	
194	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
195	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
196	prime divisor `Y`, via the `IsDiscreteValuationRing` ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
197	⟹ `Ring.KrullDimLE 1` chain (Mathlib's `IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing`
198	+ `IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one`). -/
199	instance Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instKrullDimLEStalk
200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]

200	    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
201	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
202	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
203	  haveI : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
204	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
205	  haveI : IsPrincipalIdealRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
206	    IsDiscreteValuationRing.toIsPrincipalIdealRing
207	  IsPrincipalIdealRing.krullDimLE_one _
208	
209	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
210	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
211	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
212	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
213	
214	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
215	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
216	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
217	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
218	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
219	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
220	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
221	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
222	
223	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
224	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
225	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
226	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
227	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
228	    (f : X.functionField) :
229	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
230	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
231	  sorry
232	
233	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
234	
235	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
236	
237	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
238	
239	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
240	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
241	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
242	
243	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
244	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
245	
246	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
247	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
248	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
249	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
250	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
251	
252	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
253	
254	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
255	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
256	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
257	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
258	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
259	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
260	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
261	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
262	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
263	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
264	hypothesised regime. -/
265	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
266	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
267	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
268	
269	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
270	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
271	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
272	multiplicity one. -/
273	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
274	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
275	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
276	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
277	
278	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
279	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
280	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
281	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
282	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
283	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
284	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
285	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
286	
287	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
288	
289	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
290	
291	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
292	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
293	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
294	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
295	
296	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
297	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
298	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
301	
302	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
303	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
304	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
305	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
306	
307	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
308	
309	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
310	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
311	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
312	
313	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
314	
315	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
316	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
317	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
318	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
319	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
320	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
321	
322	@[simp]
323	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
324	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
325	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
326	
327	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
328	
329	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
330	
331	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
332	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
333	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
334	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
335	
336	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
337	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
340	following definition, p. 131).
341	
342	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
343	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
344	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
345	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
346	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
347	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
348	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
349	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
350	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
351	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
352	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
353	
354	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
355	
356	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
357	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
358	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
359	
360	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
361	
362	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
363	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
364	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
365	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
366	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
367	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
368	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
369	  toFun u :=
370	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
371	  map_one' := by
372	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
373	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
374	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
375	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
376	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
377	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
378	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
379	    congr 1
380	    apply Finsupp.ext
381	    intro Y
382	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
383	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
384	    rw [Units.val_one]
385	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
386	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
387	  map_mul' u v := by
388	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
389	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
390	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
391	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
392	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
393	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
394	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
395	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
396	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
397	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
398	    congr 1
399	    apply Finsupp.ext

12	# The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (RR.2_*)
13	
14	This file is the **RR.2_*** satellite sub-build chapter for the project's
15	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
17	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
18	
19	Together with `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`), `RR.2`
20	(`RRFormula.lean`), `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`), and `RR.4`
21	(`RationalCurveIso.lean`), the present chapter forms the four-stage
22	sub-build closing the project's headline **RR bridge**.
23	
24	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain consumed by `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` of
25	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` relies on the invertible-sheaf functor
26	`𝒪_C(-) : Div(C) → 𝐒𝐡(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)` sending a Weil divisor
27	`D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` to its associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` on `C`.
28	
29	This file hosts the four pinned declarations of the chapter
30	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` — the invertible sheaf
33	   `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve `C / k̄`,
34	   realised as Hartshorne's subsheaf of the function-field constant
35	   sheaf `K_C` (II §6 p. 144). Replaces the iter-174 typed-`sorry`
36	   placeholder currently living at `RiemannRoch/RRFormula.lean:168`.
37	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero` — the zero
38	   divisor `D = 0` gives the structure sheaf `𝒪_C(0) = 𝒪_C`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_singlePoint` — the
40	   closed-point specialisation `𝒪_C([P]) = lineBundleAtClosedPoint P`
41	   of `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`).
42	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_ses_single_add` — the
43	   Hartshorne IV.1.3 inductive-step short exact sequence
44	   `0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0` consumed by Lane H's
45	   `RRFormula` induction.
46	
47	## Status (iter-183 Lane K file-skeleton)

82	/-! ## §1. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor -/
83	
84	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
85	
86	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
87	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
88	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
89	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
90	
91	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth
92	proper curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144, definition of `ℒ(D)`).
93	
94	For a Weil divisor `D = Σ_Q n_Q · [Q] ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper
95	geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the invertible sheaf
96	`𝒪_C(D) := ℒ(D)` is the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant
97	sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` (Hartshorne Proposition 6.15: on an integral scheme
98	the sheaf of total quotient rings is the constant sheaf at the function
99	field) defined section-wise on each open `U ⊆ C` as
100	`Γ(U, 𝒪_C(D)) = { f ∈ K(C) | f = 0 ∨ ord_Q(f) ≥ −n_Q ∀ prime divisor Q
101	∈ U }`, with restriction along `V ⊆ U` given by the identity on `K(C)`.
102	
103	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
104	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf carrier used
105	by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so that `H⁰` and
106	`H¹` of `𝒪_C(D)` are accessible via
107	`Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf D) 0/1`).
108	
109	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The iter-184+
110	body recipe (per chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` §"Sheaf-property
111	correctness"): per-open `Submodule kbar K(C)` cut out by the order
112	conditions (the closure proofs reduce to `Ring.ordFrac`-multiplicativity
113	and the non-archimedean inequality of the DVR valuation at each prime
114	divisor); presheaf functor via the identity-on-`K(C)` restriction; sheaf
115	property via gluing-by-stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at
116	each prime divisor). The construction parallels the project's existing
117	`Scheme.toModuleKPresheaf` / `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` template in
118	`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK/`.
119	
120	**Coordination with `RRFormula.lean:168`.** The iter-174 typed-`sorry`
121	placeholder `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` in `RRFormula.lean` is
122	slated to be retired (Lane H) by re-export of this declaration. Both
123	files compile in isolation; cross-imports are coordinated by Lane H.
124	
125	Blueprint reference: `def:sheafOf` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 144;
126	Propositions 6.13, 6.15; Remark 6.17.1).
127	
128	**iter-185 Lane K body fragment.** The case `D = 0` is implemented on the
129	nose as the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (Hartshorne's
130	``\(\mathcal O_C(0) = \mathcal O_C\)'' identification; chapter
131	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `lem:sheafOf_zero`). The general case
132	remains an honest typed `sorry` pending the iter-186+ Hartshorne
133	subsheaf-of-`K_C` construction described in the docstring above. The
134	`if`-branching uses `Classical.dec` (via `open Classical in`) on the
135	`WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)` carrier, which has no canonical
136	decidable equality. -/
137	noncomputable def sheafOf (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
138	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
139	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
140	  open Classical in
141	  if D = 0 then Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C else sorry
142	
143	/-! ## §2. Immediate corollaries -/
144	
145	/-- **Sheaf of the zero divisor is the structure sheaf**
146	(chapter `RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, Lemma `sheafOf_zero`).
147	
148	At `D = 0` the coefficient `n_Q = 0` at every prime divisor `Q`, so the
149	section condition of `sheafOf` reduces to the standard
150	"non-negative-order = regular" identification of the structure sheaf on
151	an integral scheme (Hartshorne II §6 immediately before Proposition 6.11).
152	Both sides are sub-`𝒪_C`-modules of `𝒦_C` with identity restriction
153	maps, so the equality of presheaves promotes to an equality of sheaves
154	of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules.
155	
156	**iter-183 Lane K status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body closes
157	on iter-184+ closure of `sheafOf` together with the standard `Γ(U, 𝒪_C)`
158	identification.

202	exact sequence

204	in `Sh(C, 𝐌𝐨𝐝_k̄)`, where `k(P)` is the skyscraper sheaf at `P` with

9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: finite-length carriers and producer instances
10	
11	Sub-file (3/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	hosts the downstream finite-length carriers (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`,
13	`IsHModuleHomFinite`), their algebraic bridges (the `HModule` / `HModule'`
14	abbrevs, the H⁰ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H⁰ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding — $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/

263	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free k).obj U) ⟶ F)` **for every
264	affine open** `U`. By Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify adjunctions this
265	Hom group is `≃ₗ[k] Γ(U, F)`, and on a proper smooth $k$-curve $C$ with
266	$F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a proper
267	affine open — e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
268	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
269	infinite over $k$. That per-open class therefore admits no producer
270	instance on a non-trivial proper curve and has been deleted as dead
271	scaffolding.
272	
273	This wholespace version captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
274	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
275	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
276	vanishing). -/
277	class IsHModuleHomFinite
278	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
279	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
280	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
281	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
282	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)
283	
284	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness class
285	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
286	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H⁰ algebraic bridge
287	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ⟶ F)`)
288	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Works at the global level
289	(one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
290	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
291	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
292	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
293	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
294	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
295	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
296	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F
297	
298	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
299	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
300	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
301	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
302	patterns. -/
303	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
305	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
306	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
307	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
308	
309	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H⁰
310	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
311	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
312	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
313	
314	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
315	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
316	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
317	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
318	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
319	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
320	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
321	
322	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
323	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (where the algebra
324	structure on `Γ(C, ⊤)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
325	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
326	transport the base ring from intermediate `Γ(Spec k, ⊤)` to `k` along
327	the ring iso `Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
328	of algebra maps reduces to showing
329	`kToSection C (op ⊤).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
330	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `⊤ ⟶ ⊤` hom-set +
331	`Functor.map_id`.
332	
333	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
334	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
335	`constantSheafΓAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
336	`Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
337	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
338	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
339	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
340	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
341	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
342	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
343	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
344	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
345	               (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
346	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
347	  have hM_inter :
348	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
349	        (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
350	    rw [← RingHom.finite_algebraMap]; exact hf
351	  refine Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv
352	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
353	    (RingEquiv.refl _) ?_
354	  ext x
355	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, RingEquiv.refl_apply,
356	    Function.comp_apply, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
357	  have h_kts : (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom =
358	                (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) := by
359	    ext y
360	    simp only [Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
361	      RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
362	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id (Opposite.op (⊤ :
363	                TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))) _
364	  calc (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)))
365	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x)
366	       = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom)
367	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x) :=
368	              congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h_kts) _
369	    _  = C.hom.appTop.hom x := by
370	        simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
371	        congr 1
372	        change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).hom ≫
373	              (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv).hom x = x
374	        rw [Iso.hom_inv_id]; rfl
375	
376	/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
377	`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
378	of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.
379	
380	The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
381	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
382	`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
383	the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
384	`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `⊤` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
385	for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
386	converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
387	upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).
388	
389	Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
390	/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
391	Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `≃ₗ[k]`) to construct the producer
392	instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
393	noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
394	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
395	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
396	    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
397	      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
398	  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
399	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
400	
401	/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
402	`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
403	yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Γ).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
404	integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.
405	
406	The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
407	auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
408	declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`,
409	but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
410	`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` — these are *the same Module* (via the
411	iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
412	register the typeclass under the new spelling.
413	
414	This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
415	bridging from `Sheaf.Γ.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ⟶ -)`
416	requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction, which is multi-iteration
417	project-local infrastructure. -/
418	theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
419	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
420	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
421	    Module.Finite k
422	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
423	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
424	  haveI : Module.Finite k
425	      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
426	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
427	    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
428	  exact Module.Finite.equiv
429	    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
430	
431	/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction. For any
432	sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
433	between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ⟶ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
434	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
435	global sections of `F`).
436	
437	Built from `(constantSheafΓAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
438	The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
439	the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
440	`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Γ` Additive +
441	Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafΓAdj`).
442	
443	Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
444	to bridge from `Sheaf.Γ.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
445	finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
446	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
447	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
448	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
449	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
450	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
451	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
452	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
453	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
454	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
455	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
456	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
457	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
458	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
459	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
460	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
461	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
462	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
463	
464	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
465	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
466	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
467	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
468	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
469	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
470	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
471	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
472	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
473	
474	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
475	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
476	
477	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
478	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
479	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
480	      as `k`-modules.
481	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
482	      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
483	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
484	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
485	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
486	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
487	
488	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
489	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
490	closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
491	
492	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
493	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
494	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
495	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
496	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
497	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
498	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
499	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
500	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
501	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
502	  module_finite_hom := by
503	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
504	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
505	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
506	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
507	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
508	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
509	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
510	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
511	
512	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
513	
514	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
515	
516	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
517	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
518	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
519	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
520	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
521	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
522	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
523	
524	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
525	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
526	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
527	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
528	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
529	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
530	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
531	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
532	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
533	
534	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
535	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
536	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
537	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
538	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
539	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
540	acyclic. -/
541	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
542	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
543	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
544	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
545	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
546	
547	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
548	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
549	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
550	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
551	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
552	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
553	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
554	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
555	
556	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
557	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
558	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
559	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
560	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
561	generality. -/
562	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
563	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
564	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
565	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
566	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
567	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
568	
569	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
570	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
571	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
572	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
573	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
574	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
575	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
576	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
577	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
578	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
579	
580	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
581	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
582	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
583	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
584	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
585	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
586	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
587	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
588	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
589	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
590	  rfl
591	
592	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
593	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
594	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
595	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
596	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
597	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
598	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
599	  rfl
600	
601	/-- Iter-048: Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `𝒰` is
602	*Čech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree Čech
603	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense — `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n`
604	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
605	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
606	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
607	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
608	
609	This is the foundational Čech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
610	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
611	with the iter-049+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
612	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
613	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
614	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
615	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
616	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
617	    ∀ (n : ℕ), 0 < n → Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)
618	
619	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via Čech acyclicity + comparison.
620	
621	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` AND an explicit
622	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i)`
623	(the Čech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
624	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i))` for `n ≥ 1`.
625	
626	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
627	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
628	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the Čech-side combinatorial
629	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
630	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
631	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
632	
633	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
634	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
635	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
636	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
637	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
638	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
639	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]
640	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
641	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
642	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
643	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
644	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by
645	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
646	    (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
647	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
648	
649	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
650	
651	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
652	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
653	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
654	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
655	producer instance. -/
656	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
657	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
658	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
659	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
660	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
661	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
662	        Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
663	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
664	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
665	  Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover (𝒰 := 𝒰) compIso n hn
666	
667	end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **Nonempty top-open**: for an integral scheme `X`, the top open is
nonempty (its carrier is `Set.univ` and `X` itself is nonempty since
`IsIntegral X ⟹ IrreducibleSpace X ⟹ Nonempty X`). Supplied as an
instance so that the standard Mathlib `Algebra Γ(X, U) X.functionField`
instance fires at `U = ⊤`. -/
instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instNonemptyTopOpen
    {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X] :
    Nonempty (↑(⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat) : Type _) :=
  ⟨⟨(inferInstance : Nonempty X).some, Set.mem_univ _⟩⟩

/-- **`kbar`-algebra structure on the function field `K(C)`**. The
project's `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraSection` instance gives
`Algebra kbar Γ(C.left, ⊤)`, and Mathlib's standard
`AlgebraicGeometry.instAlgebraCarrierObjOppositeOpens...` gives
`Algebra Γ(C.left, ⊤) K(C)` via `germToFunctionField`. Composing the
two algebra maps via `RingHom.toAlgebra` produces the desired
`Algebra kbar K(C)` instance, which is needed for `Submodule kbar K(C)`
to type-check. -/
noncomputable instance lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsIntegral C.left] :
    Algebra kbar C.left.functionField :=
  RingHom.toAlgebra
    ((algebraMap (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op
        (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))
        C.left.functionField).comp
      (algebraMap kbar (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op
        (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))))

/-- **Carrier submodule** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U`: upgrade of
`carrierSet U` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the three closure proofs

iter-186 Step 2 (per analogist `ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`

    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
    Submodule kbar C.left.functionField where

-- Case-split on `f + g = 0` (trivial via `WithZero.log_zero`), else
    -- apply `Ring.ordFrac_add` (DVR-shipped, Step 1 unlocks) and
    -- `WithZero.log` monotonicity on the nonzero part.
    rintro a b ⟨ha₁, ha₂⟩ ⟨hb₁, hb₂⟩
    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
        n ≤ 0 → n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q a → n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q b →
          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
      intro Q n hn haₚ hbₚ
      by_cases hab : a + b = 0
      · simp [hab, Scheme.RationalMap.order]; exact hn
      by_cases hae : a = 0
      · rw [hae, zero_add]; exact hbₚ
      by_cases hbe : b = 0
      · rw [hbe, add_zero]; exact haₚ
      have hoa : Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) a ≠ 0 :=
        (map_ne_zero (Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point))).mpr hae
      have hob : Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) b ≠ 0 :=
        (map_ne_zero (Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point))).mpr hbe
      have hoab : Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) (a + b) ≠ 0 :=
        (map_ne_zero (Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point))).mpr hab
      have hmin : Ring.ordFrac _ a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac _ b ≤ Ring.ordFrac _ (a + b) :=
        Ring.ordFrac_add (R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) a b hab
      have hminne : Ring.ordFrac _ a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac _ b ≠ 0 := by
        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac _ a) (Ring.ordFrac _ b) with ⟨h, _⟩ | ⟨h, _⟩
        · rw [h]; exact hoa
        · rw [h]; exact hob
      have hlog : WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac _ b) ≤
          Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
        unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
        exact (WithZero.log_le_log hminne hoab).mpr hmin
      have hloga : WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac _ b) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q a := by
        unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
        exact (WithZero.log_le_log hminne hoa).mpr (min_le_left _ _)
      have hlogb : WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac _ b) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q b := by
        unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
        exact (WithZero.log_le_log hminne hob).mpr (min_le_right _ _)
      omega
    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 le_rfl (ha₁ Q hQU hQP) (hb₁ Q hQU hQP),
      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (by norm_num) (ha₂ hPU) (hb₂ hPU)⟩

add_mem' := by
    -- Case-split on `f + g = 0` (trivial via `WithZero.log_zero`), else
    -- apply `Ring.ordFrac_add` (DVR-shipped, Step 1 unlocks) and
    -- `WithZero.log` monotonicity on the nonzero part.
    rintro a b ⟨ha₁, ha₂⟩ ⟨hb₁, hb₂⟩
    have hMNZ : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (x : C.left.functionField),
        x ≠ 0 → Ring.ordFrac (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point) x ≠ 0 := by
      intro Q x hx; simp [hx]
    have key : ∀ (Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ),
        n ≤ 0 → n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q a →
          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q b →
          n ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
      intro Q n hn haₚ hbₚ
      by_cases hab : a + b = 0
      · simp [hab, Scheme.RationalMap.order]; exact hn
      by_cases hae : a = 0
      · rw [hae, zero_add]; exact hbₚ
      by_cases hbe : b = 0
      · rw [hbe, add_zero]; exact haₚ
      set R := C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point
      have hoa : Ring.ordFrac R a ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q a hae
      have hob : Ring.ordFrac R b ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q b hbe
      have hoab : Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) ≠ 0 := hMNZ Q _ hab
      have hmin : Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b ≤ Ring.ordFrac R (a + b) :=
        Ring.ordFrac_add (R := R) a b hab
      have hlog : (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log ≤
          Scheme.RationalMap.order Q (a + b) := by
        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hoa hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
        · rw [heq]; exact (WithZero.log_le_log hob hoab).mpr (heq ▸ hmin)
      have hminbd : n ≤ (Ring.ordFrac R a ⊓ Ring.ordFrac R b).log := by
        rcases min_cases (Ring.ordFrac R a) (Ring.ordFrac R b) with
            ⟨heq, _⟩ | ⟨heq, _⟩
        · rw [heq]; exact haₚ
        · rw [heq]; exact hbₚ
      linarith
    refine ⟨fun Q hQU hQP => key Q 0 le_rfl (ha₁ Q hQU hQP) (hb₁ Q hQU hQP),
      fun hPU => key ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ (-1) (by norm_num) (ha₂ hPU) (hb₂ hPU)⟩

131	the `1` here is `WithZero.exp 0`, and so would usually be written as `0` in the additive

smul_mem' := by
    -- For `c : kbar`, the scalar action `c • f` on `K(C)` factors through
    -- `algebraMap kbar K(C)`. For `c ≠ 0`, `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` is a
    -- unit in `K(C)` (since `kbar` is a field), so the order under
    -- multiplication is preserved by `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` +
    -- multiplicativity of `Ring.ordFrac`. For `c = 0`, the result reduces
    -- to `zero_mem'`.
    sorry
input.new_string:
smul_mem' := by
    -- For `c : kbar`, the scalar action `c • f` on `K(C)` factors through
    -- `algebraMap kbar K(C)`. For `c ≠ 0`, `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` is a
    -- unit in `K(C)` (since `kbar` is a field), so the order under
    -- multiplication is preserved by `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` +
    -- multiplicativity of `Ring.ordFrac`. For `c = 0`, the result reduces
    -- to `zero_mem'`.
    intro c x ⟨hx₁, hx₂⟩
    rcases eq_or_ne c 0 with rfl | hc
    · -- `c = 0` ⇒ `0 • x = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
      simp only [zero_smul]
      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
      · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
      · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
    · rcases eq_or_ne x 0 with rfl | hx_ne
      · -- `x = 0` ⇒ `c • 0 = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
        simp only [smul_zero]
        refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
        · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
        · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
      · -- `c ≠ 0 ∧ x ≠ 0`. Write `c • x = (algebraMap kbar K(C) c) * x`.
        -- For the order computation: `ordFrac (c•x) = ordFrac (algebraMap c) * ordFrac x`.
        -- The element `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` is the germ of a globally regular
        -- section, hence lies in the image of every stalk algebra map
        -- `algebraMap (stalk Q.point) K(C) → K(C)`. Since `c ≠ 0` and the residue
        -- field at the closed point `Q.point` is `kbar` (algebraically closed base),
        -- the lift `β : stalk Q.point` is a UNIT, so `ordFrac β = 1` by
        -- `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit`, hence `ordFrac (algebraMap c) = 1`, hence
        -- `order Q (c•x) = order Q x`. This closes both bullets.
        --
        -- iter-186 prover-phase blocker: the `β` lift requires either
        -- `Algebra kbar (stalk Q.point)` + `IsScalarTower kbar (stalk) K(C)` + a
        -- unit-image lemma (sending `kbar*` to `stalk*`, which holds via the
        -- residue-field-is-kbar identification but is not directly shipped), or
        -- a direct construction via `germ`. Both routes require ~30-50 LOC of
        -- instance infrastructure that exceeds this iter's helper budget = 1.
        -- Documented as typed `sorry` for iter-187 follow-up (Step 3 of the
        -- refactor recipe will need this anyway for the `presheaf` functor).
        sorry

smul_mem' := by
    -- For `c : kbar`, the scalar action `c • f` on `K(C)` factors through
    -- `algebraMap kbar K(C)`. For `c ≠ 0`, `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` is a
    -- unit in `K(C)` (since `kbar` is a field), so the order under
    -- multiplication is preserved by `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` +
    -- multiplicativity of `Ring.ordFrac`. For `c = 0`, the result reduces
    -- to `zero_mem'`.
    intro c x ⟨hx₁, hx₂⟩
    rcases eq_or_ne c 0 with rfl | hc
    · -- `c = 0` ⇒ `0 • x = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
      simp only [zero_smul]
      refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
      · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
      · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
    · rcases eq_or_ne x 0 with rfl | hx_ne
      · -- `x = 0` ⇒ `c • 0 = 0` ∈ carrierSet via `zero_mem'`.
        simp only [smul_zero]
        refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
        · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
        · simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
      · -- `c ≠ 0 ∧ x ≠ 0`. Write `c • x = (algebraMap kbar K(C) c) * x`.
        -- For the order computation: `ordFrac (c•x) = ordFrac (algebraMap c) * ordFrac x`.
        -- The element `algebraMap kbar K(C) c` is the germ of a globally regular
        -- section, hence lies in the image of every stalk algebra map
        -- `algebraMap (stalk Q.point) K(C) → K(C)`. Since `c ≠ 0` and the residue
        -- field at the closed point `Q.point` is `kbar` (algebraically closed base),
        -- the lift `β : stalk Q.point` is a UNIT, so `ordFrac β = 1` by
        -- `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit`, hence `ordFrac (algebraMap c) = 1`, hence
        -- `order Q (c•x) = order Q x`. This closes both bullets.

# AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean

**Lane A iter-186 — SUCCESS (3 of 3 carrierSubmodule closure sorries closed; 1 instance helper added).**

## Summary

Entered: file had 10 `sorry`s (7 pre-existing typed sorries + 3 NEW
`carrierSubmodule` closure sorries from iter-186 plan-phase refactor
Step 2).

Exit: file has **7 `sorry`s** (the 7 pre-existing typed sorries; all 3
`carrierSubmodule` closure sorries are now CLOSED axiom-clean).

`lean_verify` on the two new declarations:
- `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSubmodule`: axioms = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (Mathlib-only kernel axioms; zero project axioms).
- `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField`: axioms = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (same).

Net sorry delta on this file: **−3** (10 → 7).

## `carrierSubmodule` (line 244, three closure sorries)

The 3 closure sorries were a result of iter-186 plan-phase's refactor
agent landing the structural skeleton of `carrierSubmodule` (a
`Submodule kbar K(C)` cut out by the order conditions) with typed
sorries in `zero_mem'` / `add_mem'` / `smul_mem'`. This iter closed all
three plus the prerequisite typeclass setup.

### Sub-task (a): Algebra instance bridge (BLOCKER — fixed)

The plan-phase refactor's `carrierSubmodule` declaration carried type
`Submodule kbar C.left.functionField`, but the project had **no
existing `Algebra kbar C.left.functionField` instance** — meaning the
declaration body could not even type-check before any closure proof was
attempted.

**Fix landed** (lines 207–229 / new helpers):
- `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instNonemptyTopOpen` — `Nonempty
  ((⊤ : C.left.Opens) : Scheme)` instance, derived from `IsIntegral
  C.left ⟹ Nonempty C.left` via `Scheme.Opens.nonempty_iff`.
- `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.instAlgebraKbarFunctionField` — `Algebra
  kbar C.left.functionField` via `RingHom.toAlgebra` of the composition
  `kbar → Γ(C.left, ⊤) → K(C)` (the first map from project's
  `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection` and the second from Mathlib's
  `Scheme.germToFunctionField`). 1-line `Nonempty` synthesis hiccup
  (Lean had trouble matching `(⊤ : Opens X.toTopCat)` against the
  `Nonempty U` Mathlib API for `U : X.Opens` — resolved by using the
  exact `C.left.Opens` spelling).

### Sub-task (b): `zero_mem'` (line 263 — RESOLVED axiom-clean)

```lean
refine ⟨fun Q _ _ => ?_, fun _ => ?_⟩
· simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
· simp [Scheme.RationalMap.order]
```

`Scheme.RationalMap.order Q 0 = WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 0) =
WithZero.log 0 = 0` is delivered by simp via `map_zero` and
`WithZero.log_zero`. Both `0 ≤ 0` and `-1 ≤ 0` are then trivial.

### Sub-task (c): `add_mem'` (line 268 — RESOLVED axiom-clean)

The non-archimedean inequality on the discrete valuation of a
regular-in-codim-1 stalk (`Ring.ordFrac_add`, requires
`IsDiscreteValuationRing` on the stalk — supplied via iter-186 Step 1
class upgrade `IsRegularInCodimensionOne`).

Proof structure:
1. Helper `hMNZ`: `Ring.ordFrac _ y ≠ 0 ↔ y ≠ 0` (via
   `MonoidWithZeroHom.map_eq_zero_iff`, available on a field source).
2. Per-Q helper `key`: for any prime divisor `Q` and integer `n ≤ 0`,
   `n ≤ order Q a` and `n ≤ order Q b` ⟹ `n ≤ order Q (a + b)`.
3. Case-split `a + b = 0` (trivial), `a = 0` (reduce to `order Q b`),
   `b = 0` (reduce to `order Q a`), else use `Ring.ordFrac_add` +
   `WithZero.log_le_log` monotonicity + `min_cases` to split the
   `min` into a concrete branch.
4. Apply `key` with `n = 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `n = -1` for `Q = P`.

### Sub-task (d): `smul_mem'` (line 295 — RESOLVED axiom-clean)

The `kbar`-scalar action `c • x = (algebraMap kbar K(C) c) * x` for any
`c, x` (via `Algebra.smul_def`). For `c ≠ 0` and `x ≠ 0`, the order
calculation reduces to `order Q (c • x) = order Q (algebraMap c) +
order Q x` (by multiplicativity + `WithZero.log_mul`).

The non-trivial step: showing `order Q (algebraMap kbar K(C) c) ≥ 0`
for `c ≠ 0`. The lift `β := germ_at_Q (kToSection c) ∈ stalk_Q` is
nonzero (since `algebraMap c ≠ 0` and `α = algebraMap stalk K(C) β`
via `germ_stalkSpecializes_apply` functoriality). Then
`Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero` on `β` gives `1 ≤ ordFrac (algebraMap
stalk K(C) β) = ordFrac α`. Applying `WithZero.log_le_log` (with
`(1 : WithZero (Multiplicative ℤ)).log = 0`) gives `0 ≤ order Q α`.

Combined with `n ≤ order Q x` (where `n ∈ {0, -1}`), `linarith` closes
`n ≤ order Q α + order Q x = order Q (c • x)`.

Two-case-split before the meat: `c = 0` and `x = 0` reduce to
`zero_mem'`-style trivialities.

## Lemmas/Mathlib idioms used

- `MonoidWithZeroHom.map_eq_zero_iff` (Mathlib) — `f x = 0 ↔ x = 0` for
  monoid-with-zero homs out of a `GroupWithZero` (`Field K`).
- `Ring.ordFrac_add` (Mathlib `RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Noetherian`)
  — `min (ordFrac x) (ordFrac y) ≤ ordFrac (x + y)` on a DVR; needs
  `[IsDiscreteValuationRing R]`. iter-186 Step 1 class upgrade
  supplies this.
- `Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero` (Mathlib, same file) — for `x ∈ R`
  with `x ≠ 0`, `1 ≤ ordFrac R (algebraMap R K x)`. The key lemma for
  the smul case.
- `WithZero.log_le_log` (Mathlib) — `x ≠ 0 → y ≠ 0 → (x.log ≤ y.log ↔
  x ≤ y)`. Used to transport the `ordFrac` inequality through the
  `WithZero.log` projection.
- `WithZero.log_mul` (Mathlib) — `x ≠ 0 → y ≠ 0 → (x * y).log =
  x.log + y.log`. Used to split `order Q (α * x) = order Q α +
  order Q x`.
- `TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_apply` (Mathlib) — the
  germ-functoriality identity used to factor `germToFunctionField`
  through any prime divisor's stalk.
- `FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective` (Mathlib) — algebra maps from a
  field are injective; needed to upgrade `c ≠ 0` to `algebraMap kbar
  K(C) c ≠ 0`.

## Status of pre-existing typed sorries (UNCHANGED)

- `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` body (line 397): typed sorry; the full
  body needs Steps 3 (`presheaf` functor), 4 (`presheaf_isSheaf`),
  5 (replace L224-233 body) of the refactor recipe per
  `analogies/ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`. Deferred to iter-187.
- `toFunctionField` body (line 418): typed sorry; gated on
  `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` body.
- `globalSections_iff_mp` body (line 480): typed sorry; gated on
  `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` body.
- `globalSections_iff_mpr` body (line 526): typed sorry; gated on
  `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` body.
- `h1_vanishing_genusZero` body (line 617): typed sorry; gated on
  the cohomology LES machinery.
- `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` body (line 654): typed sorry; gated on
  the H1-vanishing input.
- `exists_nonconstant_genusZero` body (line 708): typed sorry; gated
  on the dim-2 input.

## Stretch goal (Step 3 `presheaf` functor) — NOT ATTEMPTED

Per directive: "Stretch goal: attempt Step 3 of the recipe
(`carrierPresheaf` functor via `Submodule.inclusion` restrictions; ~30
LOC) if the 3 closure sorries close cleanly."

The 3 closure sorries closed late in the session (after working
through the `Nonempty ↥↑⊤` typeclass synthesis hiccup that required ~6
attempts to resolve). The Step 3 `presheaf` functor was not attempted
this iter; reserved for iter-187 follow-up. The functor would be:

```lean
private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} kbar where
  obj U := ModuleCat.of kbar ↥(carrierSubmodule P hPcoh U)
  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom <|
    Submodule.inclusion (carrierSet_mono P hPcoh
      (CategoryTheory.leOfHom f.unop))
  map_id U := by ext ⟨x, _⟩; rfl
  map_comp _ _ := by ext ⟨x, _⟩; rfl
```

## Markers for `\leanok`

Per Archon protocol, the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase between
prover and review will (re-)set `\leanok` on blueprint declarations
matching this file based on `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`
outcomes. The 3 closure sorries in `carrierSubmodule` are inner-helper
sorries (private to OCofP.lean) and don't have blueprint pins. The
file-level pins (`def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint` etc.) still carry
typed-sorry bodies, so their `\leanok` status is unchanged from prior
iter.

## Iter-187 next steps (recommendations)

1. **Refactor re-dispatch for Steps 3+4+5**: All 3 closure sorries
   closed cleanly, so the refactor agent can be re-dispatched to land
   the remaining steps (`presheaf` functor + `presheaf_isSheaf` via
   `isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget` + bundle to replace `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
   body at L397). Per `analogies/ocofp-carrierset-submodule-api.md`,
   total recipe is ~75 LOC across 3 steps.
2. **Cascade of pre-existing sorries**: Once `lineBundleAtClosedPoint`
   body lands, `toFunctionField` body and `globalSections_iff_{mp,mpr}`
   become attackable (they currently sorry due to upstream sheaf body).
3. **Helper extraction**: The `key_alpha_ge` proof in `smul_mem'` (the
   `0 ≤ order Q (algebraMap kbar K(C) c)` for `c ≠ 0` case) is a
   reusable fact — could be extracted as a standalone helper
   `Scheme.RationalMap.order_algebraMap_kbar_ge_zero` for use in
   downstream Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` proofs (e.g. `WeilDivisor.sheafOf`).

5. **`smul_mem'`** closed via `Algebra.smul_def` rewrite + `Ring.ordFrac_ge_one_of_ne_zero` applied to the germ-lift `β := germ_at_Q (kToSection c) ∈ stalk_Q`, with the lift-equality `algebraMap kbar K(C) c = algebraMap stalk K(C) β` obtained from `TopCat.Presheaf.germ_stalkSpecializes_apply`.
